sentences.text: No idea.
And then I'll also turn off the Slack notifications.
Hi, Hannah!
I'm sorry, I didn't realize I was on mute.
You are so fine!
Happy Friday!
Yum!
I just… I wanted to get a quick snack in between meetings, and… I… Literally got the juiciest, like.
Piece of fruit, and it's all over me now.
Oh, tell us more!
What was the fruit?
My curiosity is piqued.
It's, like, all down the front of me, it's like a nectarine.
Yay!
No!
See?
And now I'm, like, covered.
I don't know….
So much for, like… A quick little snack, and now it's all down the front of me.
Oh my gosh, Hannah, I don't know if you watched the summer I turned.
No, I do, and I didn't want to say that I was eating a peach, because I was like, ugh, I can't be that girl that's referencing this.
That… that's exactly what I thought of immediately, is that scene in the show, when you're like, it's the juiciest fruit I've ever had.
The peach from The Summer I Turned Pretty.
Oh my gosh.
We have some… I don't think Anne is on that bandwagon, but we do have some… some fans in the office of that show.
So….
Which I keep seeing the, like, I'm seeing… man, I guess this is… I'm officially in my 30s.
I officially am giving Millennial, and I don't know what's going on.
Because I'm seeing this being a trend on Instagram, but I have nowhere where, like, I don't know where it came from.
So this is a show called The Summer Returned Pretty?
Yeah, it used to be a book, so it was a book.
Yeah.
go.
Okay, are you… you must be in your late 20s, Jessica?
I'm almost 30, but I have a sister-in-law who's, like, 22, and she's like, you have to read these books and watch the show, and I'm like, okay, I guess, and now I….
Yay.
A grown, married adult worried about these teenagers falling in love.
Yeah, but see, see, I'm almost in my 30s too, but the books came out when we were teenagers.
Yes!
I read them when I was a teenager, so now….
Oh, why!
Full circle!
Oh, that's fun!
I love that for you.
That's exciting.
You're an OG, then.
I'm… I'm a newbie to it, you're an OG, Hannah.
Oh, Lord.
got.
read the books again, I'm locked in.
Yeah, Anne's probably like, what's happening?
Hi, Anne!
Bye!
Do you know any… anything about this?
I know nothing.
Sorry, I'm off-camera, I'm a little sick today, but yeah, I know nothing, I just know what Hannah gushes about on a weekly basis.
I love that, I love that.
Look, I… yes, I have to shout out my Gen Z, coworker, Lily, Jessica, who keeps me young and hip.
She does.
Yes, she does, so shout out to her, because… except on this, I have a bone to pick with her on this, because she has not brought me up to speed on the summer I turned pretty, so….
I don't know if she's in the loop, maybe, maybe she's out of the loop on this.
Totally kidding.
And Hannah Ann, it's so good to see y'all.
I actually did meet with Susan recently on, Google, our Google Business product.
I don't know if y'all remember it from last year, but….
Yes.
Google business profiles are huge this year.
I don't know if that kind of falls in line, … Excuse me, so I will talk about that here in a second.
But then, of course, also wanted to introduce you to Jessica, who's y'all's Customer Success Manager, so….
Yes.
Awesome!
It is nice to meet you guys, and glad I've now locked in someone to email about the summer I turned pretty, as well as Rentable and Maven, thanks.
So nice to meet you.
Yeah, and, yeah, Jessica has prepared a lovely deck for us.
So, yeah, kind of going into it, any questions that y'all have on y'all's rentable account?
I think our biggest thing is we're just doing budgets for next year, need to get those finalized next week.
And if there's any, like, add-ons, if we could compare pricing for adding that Google My Business feature, so if you can just kind of lay out pricing for us, I think that'd be super beneficial.
I know we've already done the research, we know kind of the tools, That we can add on, so now it's more of just, like, The financial part of it.
Okay, okay, got it.
And quick question on that, when you say that.
I guess, selfishly, specifically for Google, but then also any other tools.
Are there any tools that you are looking at outside of Rentable and Maven, regarding Google or ILS, anything like that?
I mean, we have our standard ILSs, the apartments.
com, Zillow, all of that, … That we've done our budgets for, and then… our Google Ads are with our agency, so other… I don't think we're looking to add any, like, brand new, products.
the only potential, like, newish ILS, … is Rent Cafe.
Okay.
We've been seeing some leads from them, since we used Nerdy and Rank Cafe, that's a potential new one that we've been exploring.
Got it.
I actually… yeah, I guess I didn't realize….
They had an ILS?
It's… yeah.
It's… it's… I think if you have Yardi, you just automatically are in, like, their free ILS.
Okay.
And then if you wanted, of course, like.
Add on additionals, then… discuss with them, but yeah.
Okay, cool.
Well, we love that.
It's new-ish, as of, like, the last year.
Okay, good for Alright, go off, Yardi!
Yeah.
Perfect.
Well, I'll share my screen, and then I'm gonna give it to Shelby at some point, too, so we might click back and forth.

But just wanted to put a couple things together for you guys, because you're currently on the rentable platform, That contract is up for renewal, so I do have those details for you today, so that we can talk about that, and then what that would include if you guys did, go with the Google product.
Shelby will cover all of those things.
But, just looking over your contract, we show you have about 13 properties.
Is that accurate?
Are we off by a number or two?
… Oh, I see the social boost.
Yep.
We closed on the James yesterday, so we no longer own the James.
Okay, yeah, no worries.
So, these social boosts can be updated to any properties you want.
Okay.
Since you… you have two included social boosts, and then you guys pay for an extra one, with the rentable unlimited.

So, when I… I'll email you a recap today, and I'll send you over these, and if you want to replace, since the James isn't there, we'll want to replace that with any other property that maybe is, like, needs… some help with their leasing to get some more reach out there, and then if you want to change Capstone or, House 25, same thing.
Okay, perfect.
And those social boosts, they can be changed, so it goes into effect on the first of each month, when these changes.
changes.
go in.

So you can change these as many times as you want, it'll just always go into effect that following month.
Okay.
Yeah, so you guys have rentable unlimited, two social booths, 1 paid.
For the renewal, that's on a couple slides to go over of, like, what that'll look like.

… If we… are combined…

Sable, I can definitely check on that one for you.

boulevards are together, and port sides together.

Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah.
Do some math there.
Yeah, what I'll do, too, is after this call, I'll just send you over a list of the 13 that we show.

And that way, like, you know, James is out, so we'll get that one removed, but then if there's a new one that maybe just didn't make the list, we'll make sure we get connected to that one as well, too.
Okay.
Because with that Rental Unlimited, you can… it covers everything, so if you lose a property, gain more, it's all the same price to put those in and out.
Awesome.

Couple things I'd like to go over, like, as your Customer Success Manager, it's in my name, I want to make sure you guys are successful, and you guys are, like, reaching the goals that you have, as a company.
So I like to kind of bucket how I can best help you guys into these three things.
what are your business goals?
What are you trying to achieve?
How can I help you achieve those?
So how are you measuring if something's successful?
And is there anything that you guys are facing, like, as a challenge, that I can just take note of?

Because The great thing, about Maven, and Rentable is we're always adding new things, and solving more and more problems with new products and new features, so if there's something that you guys maybe have, like, the hardest time with.
keeping an eye on that so I know.
So what… when just looking at this, does anything stand out to you guys as, like, a goal you're working on, or ways you're measuring that you're successful when it comes to rentable?
And we can even include Google in there if you have goals towards that.
Probably high conversion ratios from lead to, lead to tour.
I think that's one of the biggest things, making sure that they're converting and it's not fluff.
But yeah, and increasing lead volume in… at key points in the seasons, too.
Yeah, definitely.
Along the lines of leads, too, urgency has been kind of an issue this summer.
We're getting the leads, we're getting the tours, but the urgency to apply and lock in something has been kind of low, so that's something that I've been kind of keeping my eye on this summer.
Yeah, do you guys run specials that are, like, time-sensitive specials at your communities?
Okay, perfect.
One of the things that we can check on, too, is to make sure, are those specials filtering in on the rentable side?
That way, when they see the listings, do they see that, like, hey, this special ends in 4 days, things like that?
I'll check on those.
Do you guys… do you guys use Yardi as your property management software for all of them?
Yeah, we've… all the… all that information should feed from Rank Cafe.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah, as long as it's in there, it should be feeding directly into all the listings.
I just like to double-check on all those things, especially since I'm coming in as your new Customer Success Manager.
Make sure we're not missing any pieces.
And then I did pull for you guys, just your data from beginning of the year to the end of July, just so we can kind of see, like, where we've been, with leads.
So, over, the last 8 months, 7 months, actually, because it's the end of July, you've had about 501 leads come in.
They're always going to be bulk email leads, because nobody likes to talk on the phone anymore.
No one's calling in anymore.
That's, like, the last resort for most people, I think, at this point.
point.
And then I just wanted to kind of show you, like, where those peaks were, in leads, you'll see, like, summer is usually the hottest time that leads come in.
Everyone likes to move in the summer, and then I like to break it down also by, like, where those leads are coming from.
You guys have a pretty good 50-50 split.
You're getting things from the rentable, and our network, as well as social.
So social is a huge, lead, generator for you guys, which is awesome.
So that means those 3 social booths, they're doing their job.
They're reaching people.
We can do a lease match for you guys as well, too, if you just want to see kind of what that conversion rate is.

So if you just send over whatever time period, if you want it to be from the beginning of the year to now, or if it's just, like, maybe Q1 or Q2, you can send over any leases that were signed for all the properties or specific ones, and then my team will run a lease match for you and tell you exactly which ones came, from our network, and our listings.
Any questions about what you see here?
Nope, looks good.
Okay, perfect.

She can do her thing.

Has that kind of changed in the last year with Google Business Profiles kind of becoming increasingly more important?
I'd say it's more… we're putting it on our radar more.
Okay, okay, got it.
And, in terms of local SEO, I believe Susan mentioned, like, of course the websites are a big thing for y'all.
Is there anything else that y'all are doing for local SEO?
I… I think it's just website.

All right!
Well then, yeah, my last question on this, have y'all been to any of the big conferences this year, like NAA, or… Any of those, those big ones?
Okay, so….
Ann Murray.

Okay, so, yeah, my… my main reason asking that, is just because this year, I believe it was in February, Google placed an even larger emphasis on the importance for, your Google business profiles in terms of increasing that local SEO.
So, it's been kind of exciting for this tool this year, because it's… really taken off, and continues to be a huge, a huge resource for our partners.
So, kind of just a quick high-level overview of why your Google Business profiles are so important.
In terms of.
increase.
your local rank on Google Maps, and then increasing KPIs, like property website traffic, your Google business profiles are huge.
So, specifically, Google rewards relevant, frequent, and unique content at your Google Business profiles.
However, to do this manually is near impossible, really, even at one profile on a daily basis.
But then you multiply it across the board, and it's just impossible to get to.
And the reason why this tool is so critical is because we hear more and more that diminishing manpower seems to be a trend in terms of marketing terms nationwide.
Y'all are tasked with more, oftentimes with fewer resources, and then sometimes when on-site teams are asked to do certain things.
… then, of course, they're not able to get around to them as well.
So, cue the automation that we've done in optimizing your Google Business profiles via integration with Yardi.
So, as a reminder, what we do on the profile four times a day, or sorry, we do 4 things a day on the profile.
So, first and foremost, we're going to use your photos.
to create amenity-rich content.
We want to make sure that we're using those amenity-based keywords to ensure that when a prospective renter is looking for, like.
an apartment with a fitness center near me, you're more likely to pop up in their search.
We pull over those available floor plans with the starting price.
We actually do this with Yardi 4 times a day, so a benefit for being a Yardi user there.
And then we can also showcase your in-unit and property amenities on this products feed as well.
We help manage your Q&A feed, and then we also post about concessions when they're available.
So, wanted to pull up one of Coastal Bridge's properties, just so you could see what this looks like in real time.
So, here are gonna be those, those daily posts.
So, again, what we're going to do is we're gonna leverage your photos, and then, make sure that we speak to any amenities that are being showcased in the photos.

But yeah, what we want to do here is really just paint a picture for a prospective resident on what it's like to be, or to live at the property, since 87% of renters start their search for a new home on Google.
Any questions on that?
If y'all do ever offer concessions or specials, we will post about them once a week while they're running, and then once the special expires, we actually delete the post automatically.
So we've been seeing a lot of success there with that as well.
If we scroll down, we'll see this products feed.
So, again, what we do here is we pull over available floor plans four times a day with the starting price.
We can also, showcase the unavailable floor plans as well, but if we click in, it's going to give a little bit more in-depth look of what that floor plan entails.
And then, if they click here, it's actually going to bring us to this property website's landing page.
So, the… what we see in terms of increasing property website traffic is pretty phenomenal.
And then, lastly, if we go over to maps, what we've done for the Q&A feed is we saw, oftentimes, questions would be asked.
On multifamily Google business profiles, but then, they oftentimes would not be answered, just because, again.
this is a lot to get around to.
So, what we've done here is we now place up to 20 questions on the profile, and then we edit the answer daily, even if the question doesn't, change, or sorry, the answer doesn't change.
In updating the answer daily, we're again giving that little local SEO boost.
So these questions are going to be about policy, office hours, of course, this will also be availability, any amenities, EV chargers, so on and so forth.
So.

Yeah, wanted to show y'all that as well, and I'm curious, I know Susan liked it, and this is something that she wanted to, kind of budget into 2026, but do you all have any questions, or is this something that y'all think would be a benefit to y'all?
Yeah.
Okay.
I think it would be.
I mean, we've talked about it a bunch, so if we can try to make it work in the budgets, and… See if it gets approved.
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
Okay, great.
Well then, yeah, we can definitely talk about a few different pricing strategies.
Okay, perfect.
And then, I… I believe this was not on Susan's radar, but social media, Facebook, Instagram, are y'all doing that manually?
Yeah, we have a social media manager.
Okay, okay, got it.
Alright, perfect.

Well, yeah, we recently created, like, a pretty, robust tool, so, that's something that we can do as well on the pricing down the road, if that becomes, something that y'all are kind of interested in shopping around.
Cool.
Thank you.
Yeah, absolutely.
Okay, well, that's… that is my spiel.
Perfect.

Well, I'm gonna pull up your renewal information for you, so what I'm showing you for renewal is just if we went with rentable, if we… if you guys decide to go with ads, for the remainder, for, like, 2026, or if we even, like, started a little bit early, see how that goes, what… whatever is kind of decided there, I just want to make sure you have what the ads one would be, since it is up for renewal.
And we can always, you know, discuss, feedback, anything you guys have on it.
But right now, you'll see current contract, in the bottom left.
Is what you're looking like, so we're at month-to-month, with the ads unlimited, two included, one featured ad.

Over here on the right, I have what would be the proposed if we just went with rentable, which would be, I was able to get you guys just a 5% increase instead of the usual 10, for a 12-month contract.
So that's there, but definitely, if we're able to get Google or Maven added, we can definitely discuss, you know.
Possibly, you know, reducing the rentable, you know, 5% increase, and maybe staying flat, since you guys will be adding some cool new products, in there.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, so all.
I'm gonna send this right.
Okay.

Yep, I'll send… I know it's a ton of information, and I'm sure you have other people you need to talk to about it, so it'll be in my email recap, for you, and then… What I also like to do, is just kind of set up a cadence with you guys for us to meet, Shelby to jump in, and just, you know, continue to talk about rentable since you're actively using that product, and make sure, you know, we're meeting all your expectations there.
And then if we need, you know, to get Susan in here to talk more about the renewal and adds things, when it comes to Google, we can do that as well, too.
Perfect.
Yeah, does, is there, like, a day of the week that usually works best for you guys, if we throw something on the calendar for the upcoming month of September?
Yeah, usually either Mondays or Wednesdays are best for us.
Usually, like, Monday afternoon, early afternoon, or, like, Wednesday, pretty much any day.
Usually those days are… are good for us.
Okay.
And then Fridays are fine.
Fridays are fine, but no one likes to have a meeting on a Friday.
It's not the first choice.
So, looking at the calendar for September, I'm looking at September 10th, which is a Wednesday.
What does that look like for you guys?
Any vacations popping up that you might be going on?
You're like, absolutely not that day, that's the worst day of the week for me.
I think that's fine.
Ann gets to go on vacation next week, so….
I'll be back.
I'll be back by then.

Like, beach, mountains?
Where are you having?

That sounds like so much fun.
picture of everything.

So….
And I think our contract's up by… on September 1st.
We need to make a decision before then?

Or is that overkill?
It doesn't really matter.

Thank you so much!
